name: Mysore Pak
description: The legendary ghee-rich gram flour fudge from the royal kitchens of Mysore. Dense, crumbly, and melt-in-mouth.

name: Kaju Katli
description: Premium diamond-shaped cashew fudge with silver varak coating. A staple at every Indian celebration.

name: Rasgulla
description: Soft spongy chenna balls soaked in light sugar syrup. The pride of Bengal, now with GI tag.

name: Motichoor Ladoo
description: Tiny fried gram flour pearls bound with saffron-laced sugar syrup into perfect golden spheres.

name: Besan Barfi
description: Slow-roasted gram flour set with sugar, cardamom, and a touch of rose water. A Rajasthani classic.

name: Gulab Jamun (Tin)
description: Slow-fried milk-solid dumplings bathed in rose-cardamom sugar syrup. Comfort in every bite.

name: Halwa Puri Box
description: Suji halwa with golden puris — the quintessential Punjab weekend breakfast. Now in gift boxes.

name: Cham Cham
description: Cylindrical chhena sweets from Bengal, stuffed with malai and rolled in khoya. Delicately sweet.

name: Mysore Sandesh
description: Soft Bengali chhena patties perfumed with cardamom and saffron. Melts like a dream.

name: Imarti
description: Spiral-shaped urad dal fritters soaked in fragrant sugar syrup. A festive street favourite.

name: Churma Laddoo
description: Coarsely ground wheat deep-fried and bound with jaggery and ghee. A Rajasthani warriors' sweet.

name: Peda Box (Mathura)
description: The original saffron-cardamom khoya peda from Mathura — birthplace of Lord Krishna.

name: Anarsa
description: Rice flour discs fried in ghee and dusted with poppy seeds. A Maharashtrian Diwali essential.

name: Chikki (Mixed Box)
description: Crunchy jaggery-peanut, sesame, and cashew brittle from Lonavala. Addictive and nostalgic.

name: Tirunelveli Halwa
description: Dense wheat halwa made with pure ghee and cooked for hours. The pride of Tirunelveli for 150 years.

name: Soan Papdi
description: Gossamer-thin gram flour flakes dusted with cardamom and pistachios. The iconic Diwali sweet.

name: Bandhani Kurta
description: Hand-tied bandhani kurta in deep indigo with traditional Rajasthani dot patterns. 100% cotton.

name: Phulkari Dupatta
description: Intricate floral embroidery on mustard khaddar from Punjab. Each piece takes 3 weeks to embroider.

name: Khadi Nehru Jacket
description: Hand-spun khadi sleeveless Nehru jacket in natural off-white. A sartorial piece of Indian heritage.

name: Anarkali Suit Set
description: Floor-length Anarkali in resham silk with heavy zari border. Comes with palazzo and chiffon dupatta.

name: Ajrak Block Print Shirt
description: Hand block-printed Ajrak design shirt using natural indigo and madder dyes on fine cotton.

name: Ikat Kurta Palazzo Set
description: Pochampally double-ikat woven kurta with matching palazzo. Geometric patterns in earthy tones.

name: Patiala Salwar Set
description: Traditional pleated Patiala salwar in vibrant pink georgette with mirror-work kameez.

name: Kerala Kasavu Mundu
description: Pure cotton handloom mundu with golden kasavu border. The classic Kerala wear for Onam and weddings.

name: Sambalpuri Dress Material
description: Odisha's iconic Sambalpuri ikat fabric in bold geometric patterns. Unstitched dress material.

name: Mekhela Chadar
description: Assamese two-piece silk garment with muga silk upper and cotton mekhela. Rich traditional weave.

name: Chikankari Kurti
description: White cotton kurti with hand-done Lucknowi chikankari shadow embroidery. Timeless elegance.

name: Gota Patti Lehenga
description: Bridal lehenga with gota patti ribbon work in coral and gold. Fully lined with cotton for comfort.

name: Kantha Stitch Jacket
description: Recycled sari fabric reversible jacket with kantha running-stitch embroidery. Artisan-made.

name: Himachali Cap & Shawl Set
description: Thick woollen Himachali topi and matching shawl with geometric border. Handmade in Kullu.

name: Kanjivaram Silk Saree
description: Temple-border Kanjivaram in ruby red with gold zari peacock motifs. Pure mulberry silk, 6.3m.

name: Banarasi Tissue Saree
description: Shimmering tissue silk Banarasi with jaal brocade and kadhua butis woven in gold thread.

name: Chanderi Cotton-Silk Saree
description: Sheer Chanderi fabric with gold buti work and contrasting zari border. Light as a breeze.

name: Pochampally Ikat Saree
description: Double-ikat woven saree from Pochampally in geometric diamond patterns. Rich earthy palette.

name: Paithani Saree
description: Maharashtrian heirloom saree with tapestry-woven peacock and lotus border in real gold zari.

name: Kashmiri Sozni Saree
description: Pure silk saree with hand-done sozni needle embroidery of chinar leaves and paisley. Kashmir artisan.

name: Tant Cotton Saree
description: Light Tant handloom cotton saree in Bengali style with coloured stripes and woven border.

name: Sambalpuri Bomkai Saree
description: Odisha's Bomkai saree with temple border and traditional fish-swan motifs in contrast supplementary weft.

name: Mysore Silk Saree
description: Government-certified Mysore crepe silk saree with fine zari border and printed pallu.

name: Linen Jamdani Saree
description: Contemporary linen Jamdani with all-over floral muslin motifs. Lightweight, breathable, urban-chic.

name: Nauvari Saree
description: Traditional 9-yard Maharashtrian nauvari saree in green cotton. Worn warrior-style for festivals.

name: Phulkari Silk Saree
description: Vibrant silk base saree covered in dense Phulkari floral embroidery in orange and pink. Punjab artisan.

name: Kashmiri Kesar (Pure)
description: Grade A+ Kashmiri Mongra saffron from Pampore. 1g hand-picked threads, certified authentic.

name: Tellicherry Black Pepper
description: Large-berry Tellicherry pepper from Malabar with bold aromatic heat. World's most prized pepper.

name: Chettinad Masala Mix
description: Aromatic Chettinad blend with kalpasi, marathi mokku, and star anise. The heart of Tamil cuisine.

name: Green Cardamom (Idukki)
description: Bold fragrant green cardamom pods from Idukki hill stations. Plump, oily, intensely aromatic.

name: Guntur Red Chilli Powder
description: Fiery Guntur chilli powder ground fresh from sun-dried Sannam chillies. Rich colour, real heat.

name: Turmeric Powder (Lakadong)
description: High-curcumin Lakadong turmeric from Meghalaya. Deep orange, 6-7% curcumin content, earthy sweet.

name: Coorg Black Pepper & Coffee Set
description: Gourmet gift box — Coorg estate black pepper + single-origin Coorg coffee. Artisan packaging.

name: Rajasthani Lal Maas Masala
description: Authentic spice mix for Rajasthani red meat curry with mathania chilli and coriander.

name: Himalayan Pink Salt (Fine)
description: Mineral-rich pink salt from the Himalayan foothills. Fine-ground for everyday seasoning.

name: Amchur Powder (Raw Mango)
description: Sun-dried raw mango powder with sharp tangy flavour. Essential for North Indian chaat and curries.

name: Panchphoran Blend
description: Bengali five-spice mix — fenugreek, nigella, cumin, fennel, and mustard. Tadka in one.

name: Hing (Asafoetida) Compound
description: Premium yellow hing compound from Rajasthan. Pungent when raw, divine and savoury when cooked.

name: Spice Gift Hamper
description: Curated hamper with 8 essential Indian spices — kesar, cardamom, pepper, turmeric, coriander, and more.

name: Kashmiri Pashmina Shawl
description: Handwoven pure Pashmina shawl from Changthang goats. Hand-embroidered sozni border. 28-micron fiber.

name: Madhubani Painting (30×40cm)
description: Hand-painted Madhubani on handmade paper with natural colours by a Bihar artisan. Fish-pond motif.

name: Blue Pottery Vase (Jaipur)
description: Turquoise Jaipur blue pottery vase with white floral motifs. Persian technique adapted over centuries.

name: Dhokra Brass Elephant
description: Lost-wax cast Dhokra brass elephant from Chhattisgarh tribal artisans. Intricate geometric detailing.

name: Bidriware Vase
description: Black zinc-alloy Bidriware vase with pure silver inlay floral work. GI-tagged Bidar craft.

name: Warli Tribal Wall Art
description: White geometric figures on terracotta background — Maharashtra's ancient Warli folk art. 45×60cm.

name: Pattachitra Scroll (Puri)
description: Odisha Pattachitra scroll on treated cloth depicting Jagannath temple stories. Natural stone colours.

name: Channapatna Wooden Toys Set
description: Lacquered ivory wood spinning tops, rattles, and animals from Karnataka's toy town. Non-toxic.

name: Rogan Art Wall Panel
description: Castor-oil based Rogan paint on black cloth — Gujarat's near-extinct art form. Tree of life motif.

name: Terracotta Jewellery Set
description: Hand-moulded terracotta earrings and necklace with natural pigments. Lightweight, earthy, artisan.

name: Naga Tribal Necklace
description: Warrior beaded necklace from Nagaland in red and black seeds with brass hook clasp. Collector piece.

name: Bamboo Lamp Shade
description: Hand-woven bamboo pendant lamp shade from Assam. Warm amber glow, eco-friendly, minimalist.

name: Marble Inlay Photo Frame
description: White Makrana marble frame with pietra dura semi-precious stone floral inlay. Taj Mahal technique.

name: Gond Painting (Canvas)
description: Madhya Pradesh tribal Gond art on canvas — intricate dot-line work depicting forest wildlife.

name: Coir Doormat (Handwoven)
description: Natural coir fibre doormat hand-woven in Kerala with 'Swagatam' (Welcome) motif in natural dyes.

name: Kondapalli Wooden Bullock Cart
description: Andhra Pradesh's famous Kondapalli toy — hand-carved light tella poniki wood with natural lac colours.

name: Kashmiri Papier-Mâché Box
description: Hand-painted papier-mâché jewellery box with gold-leaf detailing on black lacquer. Kashmir artisan.

name: Brass Puja Thali Set
description: Handcrafted brass thali with diyas, bell, incense holder, and sindoor box. Festival puja set.

name: Leather Mojari Shoes (Pair)
description: Hand-stitched Rajasthani leather mojari with gold zari embroidery on pointed toe. Festival footwear.

name: Block Print Cushion Cover Set
description: Set of 5 hand block-printed cushion covers in Sanganer print. Cotton, 45×45cm, zipper closure.

name: Tanjore Painting (Ganesha)
description: Traditional Thanjavur painting of Ganesha with 24K gold foil and precious stone embellishments.

name: Kalamkari Table Runner
description: Pen-drawn Kalamkari table runner in mythological design using natural vegetable dyes. Srikalahasti.

name: Soapstone Incense Holder
description: Hand-carved Rajnandgaon soapstone incense stand with elephant motif. Natural cool grey finish.

name: Handmade Paper Journal
description: A5 journal with Jaipur handmade deckle-edge paper, block-print cover, and cotton-rag pages. 160pp.

name: Gond Art Mug Set
description: Set of 2 ceramic mugs printed with authentic Gond tribal art motifs. Dishwasher safe, 350ml.

name: Kashmiri Walnut Kernels
description: Light golden walnut halves from the orchards of Wular Lake valley. Thin-skinned, sweet, and rich in omega-3s. Grade A export quality.

name: Mamra Badam (Himalayan Almonds)
description: Rare mamra almonds from Himalayan foothills — small, intensely flavoured, and oil-rich. Not the California kind. The real thing.

name: Khajoor (Premium Dates Box)
description: Soft, caramel-sweet dates from Rajasthan. Perfect for Ramzan, puja prasad, or daily snacking. No preservatives added.

name: Mixed Dry Fruit Gift Hamper
description: Premium gifting hamper with Kashmiri walnuts, almonds, cashews, pistachios, raisins, and apricots in an embossed gift box.

name: Kismis & Anjeer Combo
description: Seedless green raisins from Nashik paired with soft, naturally-dried figs from Maharashtra. Classic combination for mithai and cooking.

name: Darjeeling First Flush FTGFOP1
description: March-April first flush from Margaret's Hope estate, Darjeeling. Muscatel notes, bright golden liquor. The champagne of teas.

name: Assam CTC Breakfast Tea
description: Bold, malty Assam CTC from Jorhat gardens. Makes the perfect cutting chai — full-bodied, brews in 3 minutes, holds up to milk.

name: Nilgiri Blue Mountain Oolong
description: Hand-rolled oolong from the misty Ooty hills. Light floral bouquet, smooth finish. Less known than Darjeeling but equally extraordinary.

name: Masala Chai Spice Blend
description: Hand-ground masala chai mix: cardamom, ginger, cinnamon, black pepper, and star anise. Add half a teaspoon to any chai for instant magic.

name: Kashmiri Kahwa Tea
description: Traditional Kashmiri green tea with saffron strands, cinnamon, cardamom, and rose petals. Served at every Kashmiri wedding and celebration.

name: Aam Ka Achaar (Mango Pickle)
description: Raw Uttar Pradesh mangoes, mustard oil, and a secret masala. The kind your nani made. Aged minimum 3 months before packing.

name: Andhra Avakaya Pickle
description: The fierce, unapologetic Andhra mango pickle. Guntur red chilli, mustard powder, raw mango chunks in sesame oil. Made in Vijayawada since 1952.

name: Gongura Chutney
description: Andhra's soul food — tangy gongura leaves with red chilli, garlic, and urad dal. Goes on everything: rice, roti, dosa.

name: Mixed Pickle Gift Hamper
description: Curated pickle box: mango, lemon, garlic, mixed vegetable, and green chilli. Five miniature jars — perfect housewarming or Diwali gift for food lovers.

name: Kerala Coconut Chutney Powder
description: Instant dry chutney powder — roasted coconut, urad dal, curry leaves, and dry chilli. Mix with oil or water, or eat dry with idli-dosa.

name: Kundan Earrings (Festive)
description: Traditional Kundan-set earrings with meenakari enamel back. Lightweight yet statement-making. Pairs beautifully with Kanjivaram or Banarasi sarees.
material: Brass with gold foil, kundan stones

name: Oxidised Silver Choker Necklace
description: Handmade oxidised silver-finished choker with tribal motifs. Pairs with casual and ethnic wear equally well. Adjustable length.

name: Temple Jewellery Set
description: South Indian temple-style gold-toned set featuring goddess motifs and antique finish. Ideal for bharatanatyam, weddings, and Navratri.

name: Lac Bangles Set (Rajasthani)
description: Handmade lac resin bangles with mirrorwork and stone embellishments. A Rajasthani wedding staple. Set of 12.
material: Lac resin with mirror and stone inlay

name: Meenakari Maang Tikka
description: Intricate meenakari enamelled maang tikka in cobalt blue and red. Worn at the hair parting. A bridal essential in Rajasthan.
